content: I will now replace the old flash sale UI block with the interactive spinning wheel widget, needles, success banners, dynamic coupon tickers, exclusive unlocked items list, and re-play hooks.
thinking:
**Designing the Visuals**

Okay, I've got a plan to replace those lines with the "La Ruleta de la Suerte 🎰" section. My immediate focus is on crafting a visually stunning UI for it. I'm brainstorming the design right now, starting with the section title. I need to make it pop!


**Visualizing the Wheel**

I'm now zeroing in on the "Ruleta de la Suerte" wheel's visual structure. I'm building it as a container with circular inner components and animated rotations. I've got the basic visual structure in place with six colored segments using custom gradients, and I'm figuring out how to render the round appearance of the wheel segments. I'm also planning to overlay segment labels.


**Refining the UI Elements**

I'm now diving deep into refining the UI elements for the "La Ruleta" segment, focusing on the visual hierarchy and user interaction. I'm especially excited about the "won" state, where a spectacular success card will appear! I'm planning to use a massive highlighted reward card to display the winning prize, with a description and a real-time countdown timer for added urgency. I also want to ensure a smooth transition from the spinning animation to the winning state.

args.Description: "Replace flash UI section with the interactive Lucky Wheel gamified section in index.tsx"
args.Instruction: "Replace the flash sale section render block in index.tsx with the interactive lucky wheel segments and won-prize unlock panels"
